intro: Uragoga paradoxa, also known as the 'Paradox Tree,' is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Annonaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its potential health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga paradoxa include alkaloids, terpenoids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
